title: Repository, Python Runtime, Dependencies, Parsers, and Reproducibility · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reproducible crawler repository baseline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, data fixtures, licenses, secrets, and a recovery anchor before editing.
Record Python implementation and version, platform, SSL backend, event-loop assumptions, encoding environment, and supported runtime matrix.
Pin direct and resolved dependencies, verify hashes and sources, review licenses and advisories, and prove clean installation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug site-quality team handles case 6286 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile revision, runtime, dependency, parser, license, advisory, and clean-environment evidence; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Environment Reproducibility
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-environment-reproducibility-epuad2" that uses case 2784 to reconcile revision, runtime, dependency, parser, license, advisory, and clean-environment evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Environment Reproducibility.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Environment Reproducibility into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Environment Reproducibility, keep Environment Reproducibility connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-environment-reproducibility-epuad2
# Competency: scraper-environment-reproducibility.
# Case 2784: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_environment_reproducibility_epuad2(declared_dependencies: int = 5, resolved_dependencies: int = 5, clean_installs: int = 3, parser_profiles: int = 4, unresolved_advisories: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_epuad2 = "epuad2-2daupe-2784"
    assert evidence_variant_epuad2
    evidence_axes_epuad2 = (
        "authorizationepuad2",
        "url2784",
        "response2daupe",
        "treeepuad22784",
        "frontier2784epuad2",
        "report2daupe2784",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_epuad2)) == 6
    if declared_dependencies < 1 or resolved_dependencies < 1:
        return False, resolved_dependencies, "dependency-shape"
    if resolved_dependencies != declared_dependencies:
        return False, resolved_dependencies, "dependency-drift"
    if clean_installs < 2 or parser_profiles < 3:
        return False, clean_installs, "reproduction-gap"
    if unresolved_advisories > 0:
        return False, unresolved_advisories, "advisory-gap"
    assert resolved_dependencies == declared_dependencies and clean_installs >= 2
    return True, clean_installs, "reproducible-crawler-baseline"
